name: Anal Furunculosis (Perianal Fistulas)
description: Anal furunculosis is a progressive ulcerative condition causing painful fistulous tracts and tissue destruction in the perianal region. It is immune-mediated in nature and strongly associated with German Shepherds. It causes severe pain and quality-of-life impairment.
symptoms: Visible ulcers or tracts around anus
treatment_overview: Cyclosporine is the treatment of choice, inducing remission in most cases. Tacrolimus topical gel is used in mild cases. Surgery may be required to remove persistent tracts. Lifelong management is often needed.
prevention_tips: Regular tail and perianal hygiene may reduce severity
Low-fibre diet reduces trauma during defecation
